elements.sent-2411-001: We prove that while the Hausdorff distance between the spectra of the Weyl quantization of the above symbols in a neighbourhood of $\delta=0$ is still of the order $\sqrt{|\delta|}$, the distance between their spectral edges behaves like $|\delta|^\nu$ with $\nu\in[1/2,1)$ depending on the rate of decay of the second derivatives of $F$ at infinity.
elements.sent-2411-002: In \cite{CP3 we {considered the regularity of {\ investigated how the spectrum of a class of $\Psi$DO {\ changes (seen as a subset of the real line) with respect to a family of slowly varying dilation-type perturbations.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-003: The motivation came from a related problem discussed in \cite{GRS in which $F$ was an affine function.}
elements.sent-2411-004: In this note we present some results that may be obtained when one eliminates the 'slow variation' hypothesis {\ and the perturbed symbols are of the form $a\big(x+\delta\,F(x),\xi\big)$ instead of $a\big(x+F(\delta \, x),\xi\big)$.}
elements.sent-2411-005: We note that when $F$ is affine as in \cite{GRS , the two problems are essentially the same .}
elements.sent-2411-006: We shall use the multi-index conventions of \cite{H-3 .}
elements.sent-2411-007: Let: \begin{equation}\label{dc1} \nu_{n,m}(a)\,:=\,\underset{|\alpha|\leq n}{\max}\,\underset{|\beta|\leq m}{\max}\,\sup_{(x,\xi) \in \R^{2d}}|\partial_x^\alpha\partial_\xi^\beta a|,\quad \forall (n,m)\in N\timesN\end{equation} and $S^0_{0,0 (\R^{d}\times\R^d)$ \cite{H-3 the set of smooth functions satisfying \begin{equation}\label{dc1-1} \nu_{n,m}(a)\,<\infty,\quad \forall (n,m)\in N\timesN.\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-008: We shall consider some real H\"ormander symbol $a(x,\xi)$ of class $S^0_{0,0 (\R^{d}\times\R^d)$.}
elements.sent-2411-009: We shall denote by $\big(\cdot,\cdot\big)_{L^2(\R^d)}$ the scalar product in $L^2(\R^d)$ (considered antilinear in the first variable), with the quadratic norm denoted simply by $\|\cdot\|$ and we shall use the notation $\big\langle\cdot,\cdot\big\rangle_{\mathscr{S (\mathcal{V ) :\mathscr{S ^\prime(\mathcal{V )\times\mathscr{S (\mathcal{V )\rightarrow\mathbb{C}$ for the canonical bilinear duality map for tempered distributions on the real finite dimensional Euclidean space $\mathcal{V}$.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-010: Following \cite{H-3 we define the Weyl quantization of the symbol $a\in\,S^0_{0,0 (\R^{d}\times\R^d)$ as the operator: \beq \big(\Op^w(a)\varphi\big)(x)=(2\pi)^{-d}\int_{\R^d dy\int_{\R^d d\eta\,e^{i<\eta,x-y>}\,a\big((x+y)/2,\eta\big)\,\varphi(y),\quad\forall\varphi\in\mathscr{S (\R^d),\ \forall x\in\R^d.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-011: Let $\mathfrak{K [a]\in\mathscr{S ^\prime(\R^d\times\R^d)$ be the distribution kernel of $\Op^w(a)$ (see \cite{Schw ); it may be computed by the following formula: \beq \mathfrak{K [a]:=(2\pi)^{-d/2}\big((\bb1\otimes\mathcal{F ^-)a\big)\circ\Upsilon \eeq where $\Upsilon:\R^d\times\R^d\ni(x,y)\mapsto\big((x+y)/2,x-y\big)\in\R^d\times\R^d$ is a bijection with Jacobian -1 and: \beq \big(\mathcal{F ^-\varphi\big)(v)\,:=\,(2\pi)^{-d/2}\int_{\R^d d\xi\,e^{i<\xi,v>}\,\varphi(\xi),\quad\forall\varphi\in\mathscr{S (\R^d),\ \forall v\in\R^d \eeq is the inverse Fourier transform.}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: With a slight abuse, we can write the following explicit formula: \beq \mathfrak{K [a](z+v/2,z-v/2)\equiv\widetilde{\mathfrak{K [a](z,v):=(2\pi)^{-d}\int_{\R^d d\eta\,e^{i<\eta,v>}\,a(z,\eta).. \eeq.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-014: Given a distribution kernel $\widetilde{\mathfrak{K [a]$ as in the above Proposition \ref{P-distr-ker and using the operator-norm estimation in the Calderon-Vaillancourt Theorem for its associated H\"{o rmander symbol $a=(\bb1\otimes\mathcal{F )\widetilde{\mathfrak{K [a]\in S^0_{0,0 (\Rd\times\Rd)$, our main criterion for $L^2(\R^d)$-boundedness of the associated linear operator in $L^2(\R^d)$ will be the boundedness {\ of at least one of the seminorms : \beq \nu_{n,m}\big((\bb1\otimes\mathcal{F )\widetilde{\mathfrak{K }\big),\quad\min(n,m)\geq3d+4.}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-016: To any real-valued symbol $a\in S^0_{0,0 (\R^d\times\R^d)$ we associate the perturbed symbols:*{-0,3cm \beq\begin{split}\nonumber a[F]_\delta(x,\xi)\,:=\,a\big(x+\delta\,F(x),\xi\big).}}
elements.sent-2411-017: We evidently have the inequalities: \begin{equation}\nu_{n,m}(a[F]_\delta)\,\leq\,C_n(\delta,F)\nu_{n,m}(a),\quad\forall(n,m)\inN\timesN,\end{equation} with $C_n(\delta,F)$ depending on the $\sup$-norm of the derivatives of $F$ up to order $n-1$, uniformly in $\delta\in(0,1]$.
elements.sent-2411-018:
We shall use the short-hand notations (for $|\delta|\leq1$): \begin{equation}\label{F-intkerdelta} K_\delta:=\Op^w\big(a[F]_\delta\big)\inB\big(L^2(\R^d)\big),\quadK_\delta:=\widetilde{K}\big[a[F]_\delta\big]\in\mathscr{S}^\prime(\R^d\times\R^d);\end{equation}
\begin{equation}\label{F-spedgedelta} E_+(\delta):=\sup\,\sigma(K_\delta).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-019: The Hausdorff distance: $\dH(A,B):=\max\Big\{\underset{t\in A {\sup}\,\dist(t,B)\,,\,\underset{t\in B {\sup}\,\dist(t,A)\Big\}$ for $A,B$ subsets of $\mathbb{C}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-020: There exists $C(a,F)>0$ such that for $|\delta|\leq1$ we have the estimation: \begin{equation}\dH\Big(\sigma\big(K_\delta\big)\,,\,\sigma\big(K_0\big)\Big)\,\leq\,C(a,F)\sqrt{|\delta|}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-021: Counter-examples from the literature show that this estimation is 'sharp', i.e. spectral gaps of order $\sqrt{|\delta|}$ may be created by these type of perturbations.
elements.sent-2411-022: From Theorem 1.5 in \cite{CP3 and some other similar results from the literature, one may expect a more regular behaviour of the spectral edges {\ seen as functions of $\delta$.}}
elements.sent-2411-023: By spectral edges we understand quantities like $\sup \sigma(K_\delta)$, $\inf \sigma(K_\delta)$, or the extremities of the possible inner gaps in the spectrum .
elements.sent-2411-024: In this situation we obtain the following result depending on the decay at infinity of the second order derivatives of the 'perturbing function' $F\in C^\infty_1(\Rd;\Rd)$.
elements.sent-2411-025: Suppose that $\big|\big(\partial_{x_j}\partial_{x_k F\big)(x)\big|\leq\,C<x>^{-(1+\mu)}$ for some $C>0$, $\mu>0$ and for any pair of indices $(j,k)$.}
elements.sent-2411-026: Then there exists $C(a,F)>0$ and $\delta_0>0$ such that for $|\delta|\leq\delta_0$ we have the estimation: \begin{equation}\big|\,E_\pm(\delta)\,-\,E_\pm(0)\,\big|\,\leq\,C(a,F)|\delta|^{(1+\mu)/(2+\mu)}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-027: The main idea of the proof is to construct a 'quasi-resolvent" (see \eqref{DF-qRez-delta ) and use the unitarity of $x$-translations and localization around a lattice of points in $\R^d_x$ in order to control the possible linear growth of $F$.}

elements.sent-2411-034: Thus, if we can impose by some localization procedure, a bound uniform in $(z,\gamma)\in\R^d\times\Z^d$ for the factor $\delta^{1-\kappa F(z)-\gamma$ and its $z$-derivatives then we may obtain a decaying factor $\delta^\kappa$ going to 0 with $\delta\geq0$.}
elements.sent-2411-035: We are thus lead to consider the following partition of unity:.
elements.sent-2411-036: We fix a function $g\in C^\infty_0\big(\R^d;[0,1]\big)$ such that: $\underset{\gamma\in\Z^d {\sum g(z-\gamma)^2=1,\ \forall z\in\R^d$.}}
elements.sent-2411-037: For any $\gamma\in\Z^d$ we define the cut-off function: $g[F_\delta]_\gamma(z):=g\big(\delta^{(1-\kappa) F(z)-\gamma\big)$.}
elements.sent-2411-038: Given $\gamma\in \Z^d$ we denote by $V_\gamma$ the set of all $\gamma'\in \Z^d$ with the property that the support of $g[F_\delta]_{\gamma^\prime}$ has a non-empty overlap with the support of $g[F_\delta]_\gamma$, including $\gamma'=\gamma$.
elements.sent-2411-039: Denote by $\mathfrak{n _g\in\mathbb{N}\setminus\{0\}$ the cardinal of $V_\gamma$, notice that it is clearly independent of $\gamma$ and $\delta$ and that: \begin{equation}\underset{\gamma\in\Z^d}{\sum}\big[g[F_\delta]_\gamma(z)\big]^2=1,\ \forall z\in\R^d, z\in\supp\,g[F_\delta]_\gamma\ \Longrightarrow\ \exists L>0,\ \big|\delta^{(1-\kappa)}F(z)-\gamma\big|\,\leq\,L.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-040: Finally let us denote by $G[F_\delta]_\gamma$ the self-adjoint, bounded operator of multiplication with $g[F_\delta]_\gamma$ in $L^2(\R^d)$.
elements.sent-2411-041: Obviously $G[F_\delta]_\gamma=\Op^w\big(g[F_\delta]_\gamma\big)$ for $g[F_\delta]_\gamma\in S^0_{0,0 (\R^d\times\R^d)$ a symbol independent of the second variable.}
elements.sent-2411-042: Let us fix any $\z\notin\sigma\big(K_0\big)$ and define: \beq T_\gamma(\z;\delta):=\tau_{-z_\gamma(\delta)}\,\big(K_0-\z\bb1\big)^{-1}\tau_{z_\gamma(\delta) ,\quad \widetilde{T (\z;\delta):=\underset{\gamma\in\Z^d {\sum G[F_\delta]_\gamma\,T_\gamma(\z;\delta)\,G[F_\delta]_\gamma.. \eeq.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-043: Unitarity of translations and the functional calculus for self-adjoint operators imply that for any $\delta\in[0,1]$ we have the estimation: \begin{equation}\big\|T_\gamma(\z;\delta)\big\|_{B(L^2(\R^d))}\,=\,\big\|\big(K_0-\z\bb1\big)^{-1}\big\|_{B(L^2(\R^d))}\,\leq\,\dist\big(\z,\sigma(K_0)\big)^{-1},\ \forall\gamma\in\Z^d.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-044: For any $\delta\in[0,1]$ the series in \eqref{DF-qRez-delta is convergent in the strong operator topology and we have the estimation \begin{equation}\big\|\widetilde{T}(\z;\delta)\big\|_{B(L^2(\R^d))}\,\leq\,\sqrt{(n_g+1)/2}\big\|\big(K_0-\z\bb1\big)^{-1}\big\|_{B(L^2(\R^d))}\,\leq\,\sqrt{(n_g+1)/2}\dist\big(\z,\sigma(K_0)\big)^{-1}.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-045: The convergence and the estimation in the Lemma are then evident.

elements.sent-2411-060: We intend to estimate the difference $\mathcal{E _+(\delta)-\mathcal{E _+(0)$ for $\delta>0$ small enough, and begin by making explicit the defining formula \eqref{F-spedgedelta : \beq\begin{split \mathcal{E _+(\delta) =\underset{\|\phi\|_{L^2(\R^d) =1 {\sup}\big(\phi\,,\,\Op\big(a[F]_\delta\big)\,\phi\big)_{L^2(\R^d) =\underset{\|\phi\|_{L^2(\R^d) =1 {\sup}\big\langle\mathfrak{K}\big[a[F]_\delta\big]\,,\,\overline{\phi}\otimes\phi\big\rangle_{\mathscr{S (\R^d\times\R^d) =\underset{\|\phi\|_{L^2(\R^d) =1 {\sup}\big\langle\mathfrak{K _\delta\,,\,(\overline{\phi}\otimes\phi)\circ\Upsilon^{-1}\big\rangle_{\mathscr{S (\R^d\times\R^d) .. \end{split}\eeq.}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}

elements.sent-2411-065: In fact we shall choose some function $\chi\in C^\infty_0(\R^d)$, taking values in $[0,1]$, having support in the ball $|v|\leq R$ and being equal to 1 on the ball $|v|\leq r$, for some strictly positive $r<R$.
elements.sent-2411-066: Let us first estimate the integral on the unbounded region, for any $\kappa\in(0,1)$ and $N\in\mathbb{N}$: \begin{equation}\left|\int_{\R^d}du\int_{\R^d}dz\,\mW_\kappa(z-u)\int_{\R^d}dv\,\overline{\phi(z+v/2)}\phi(z-v/2)\,K_0\big(z+\delta F(u),v\big)<v>^N<v>^{-N}[1-\chi(\kappa\,v)]\right| \leq\,\kappa^{N}\Big(r^{-N}\underset{z\in\R^d}{\sup}\,\underset{|v|\geq r}{\sup}<v>^N\big|K_0\big(z,v\big)\big|\Big)\,\|\phi\|_{L^2(\R^d)}^2\,\leq\,C_r(a)\,\kappa^N\,\|\phi\|_{L^2(\R^d)}^2.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-067: On the support of $\chi$ we shall use the second formula in \eqref{F-weight-dec in order to write that: \begin{equation}\int_{\R^d}du\int_{\R^d}dz\,\mW_\kappa(z-u)\int_{\R^d}dv\,\overline{\phi(z+v/2)}\phi(z-v/2)\,K_0\big(z+\delta F(u),v\big)\,\chi(\kappa\,v)= \nonumber \hspace*{4cm}=\Big(\widetilde{\phi}\,,\,\big(\bb1_{\R^d_u}\otimes\tau_{-\delta F(u)}\big)\big(\bb1_{\R^d_u}\otimes\Op^w(a_\kappa)\big)\big(\bb1_{\R^d_u}\otimes\tau_{\delta F(u)}\big)\widetilde{\phi}\Big)_{L^2(\R^d;L^2(\R^d))}\end{equation} with: \begin{equation}\big(K[a_\kappa]\circ\Upsilon\big)(z,v): =\big((\kappa/4\pi)^{-d/2}\mW_\kappa(v)\big)^{-1/4}K_0(z,v)\,\chi(\kappa\,v)=e^{|\kappa\,v|^2/16}\,\chi(\kappa\,v)\,K_0(z,v), \nonumber =K_0(z,v)\,\chi(\kappa\,v)\,+\,\kappa^2\int_0^1ds\,(|v|^2/16)\,e^{s|\kappa\,v|^2/16}\,\chi(\kappa\,v)\,K_0(z,v), \widetilde{\phi}: =\big(\tau_{-u}\mW_\kappa\big)\,\phi\in\,L^2\big(\R^d_u;L^2(\R^d_z)\big).\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-068: We notice that we have a unitary map \beq L^2(\R^d)\ni\phi\,\mapsto\,\widetilde{\phi :=\big(\tau_{-u}\mW_\kappa\big)\,\phi\in\,L^2\big(\R^d_u;L^2(\R^d_z)\big) \eeq and the equality (taking into account the unitarity of translations): \begin{equation}\Big(\widetilde{\phi}\,,\,\big(\bb1_{\R^d_u}\otimes\tau_{-\delta F(u)}\big)\big(\bb1_{\R^d_u}\otimes\Op^w(a_\kappa)\big)\big(\bb1_{\R^d_u}\otimes\tau_{\delta F(u)}\big)\widetilde{\phi}\Big)_{L^2(\R^d;L^2(\R^d))}=\Big(\phi\,,\,\Op^w(a_\kappa)\phi\Big)_{L^2(\R^d)}.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-069: Thus if we change the Hilbert space $L^2(\R^d)$ with the Hilbert space $L^2\big(\R^d;L^2(\R^d)\big)$ via the above unitary map we may conclude that: \begin{equation}\int_{\R^d}du\int_{\R^d}dz\,\mW_\kappa(z-u)\int_{\R^d}dv\,\overline{\phi(z+v/2)}\phi(z-v/2)\,K_0\big(z+\delta F(u),v\big)\,\chi(\kappa\,v)= \hspace*{0.2cm}=\Big(\phi\,,\,\Op^w(a_\kappa)\phi\Big)_{L^2(\R^d)}\ =\ \Big(\phi\,,\,\Op^w(a_\chi)\phi\Big)_{L^2(\R^d)}\,+ \nonumber \hspace*{0.6cm}+\kappa^2\int_{\R^d}dz\int_{\R^d}dv\,\overline{\phi(z+v/2)}\phi(z-v/2)\Big(\int_0^1ds\,e^{s|\kappa\,v|^2/16}\Big)\,(|v|^2/16)\,K_0\big(z+\delta F(u),v\big)\,\chi(\kappa\,v)\end{equation} where we have put into evidence the symbol $a_\chi\in S^0_{0,0 (\R^d\times\R^d)$ associated to the integral kernel $\mathfrak{K [a](x,y)\chi\big(\kappa(x-y)\big)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-070: Then we may control the factor $(|v|^2/16)$ using the rapid decay of $\mathfrak{K _0$ with respect to the variable $v\in\R^d$ and write that $\exp\big(s|\kappa\,v|^2/16\big)\chi(\kappa\,v)\leq\,\exp(R^2/16)$.}
elements.sent-2411-071: Finally we use once again the estimation on the support of $1-\chi$: \begin{equation}\big(\phi\,,\,\Op(a_\chi)\,\phi\big)_{L^2(\R^d)}\,= \hspace*{0,5cm}=\,\big(\phi\,,\,\Op(a)\,\phi\big)_{L^2(\R^d)}\,-\,\int_{\R^d}dz\,\int_{\R^d}dv\,\overline{\phi(z+v/2)}\phi(z-v/2)\,K_0\big(z,v\big)[1-\chi(\kappa\,v)] \Big|\big(\phi\,,\,\Op(a)\,\phi\big)_{L^2(\R^d)}\,-\,\int_{\R^d}dz\,\int_{\R^d}dv\,\overline{\phi(z+v/2)}\phi(z-v/2)\,K_0\big(z,v\big)[1-\chi(\kappa\,v)]\Big|\,\leq \hspace*{2cm}\leq\,\kappa^{N}\Big(r^{-N}\underset{z\in\R^d}{\sup}\,\underset{|v|\geq r}{\sup}<v>^N\big|K_0\big(z,v\big)\big|\Big)\,\|\phi\|_{L^2(\R^d)}^2\,\leq\,C(a)\,\kappa^N\,\|\phi\|_{L^2(\R^d)}^2.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-072: This Proposition clearly implies the estimation: \beq\ \big|\widetilde{\mathcal{E _+ (\delta)\,-\,\mathcal{E _+(0)\big|\,=\,\mathcal{O (\kappa^2).. \eeq.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-073: There exists $C(a,F)>0$ such that for any $(\kappa,\theta)\in(0,1)\times(0,1)$ and for any $\phi\in\,L^2(\R^d)$: \beq\begin{split \big\langle\mathfrak{K _\delta\,,\,(\overline{\phi}\otimes\phi)\circ\Upsilon^{-1}\big\rangle_{\mathscr{S (\R^d\times\R^d)}\,-\,\big\langle\mW[\mathfrak{K _\delta]\,,\,(\overline{\phi}\otimes\phi)\circ\Upsilon^{-1}\big\rangle_{\mathscr{S (\R^d\times\R^d) = *{7cm =\,C(a,F)\,\|\phi\|_{L^2(\R^d) ^2\,\big(\delta/\theta+\delta\kappa^{-2}\theta^{1+\mu +\delta^2\kappa^{-2}\big).. \end{split}\eeq.}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-074: We shall need a second cut-off, this time on the perturbing field $F\in C^\infty_1(\R^d)$.
elements.sent-2411-075: Let us consider the same function $\chi\in C^\infty_0(\R^d)$ as in the proof above and the weighted one $\chi_\theta(z):=\chi(\theta\,z)$ for some cut-off parameter $\theta\in(0,1]$.
elements.sent-2411-076: Then we define: \beq F_\theta:=\chi_\theta\,F,\qquad F_\theta^\bot:=(1-\chi_\theta)\,F \eeq and the corresponding integral kernels $\mathfrak{K _\delta^\circ$ and $\mathfrak{W [\mathfrak{K _\delta]^\circ$ with $F$ replaced by $F_\theta$ and respectively $\mathfrak{K _\delta^\bot$ and $\mathfrak{W [\mathfrak{K _\delta]^\bot$ with $F$ replaced by $F_\theta^\bot$.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-077: We have the evident estimations: \begin{equation}\big\langleK_\delta^\circ\,,\,(\overline{\phi}\otimes\phi)\circ\Upsilon^{-1}\big\rangle_{\mathscr{S}(\R^d\times\R^d)}-\big\langleK_0\,,\,(\overline{\phi}\otimes\phi)\circ\Upsilon^{-1}\big\rangle_{\mathscr{S}(\R^d\times\R^d)}= =\int_{\R^d}dz\int_{\R^d}dv\,\overline{\phi(z+v/2)}\phi(z-v/2)\,\Big[K_0\big(z+\delta F_\theta(z),v\big)-K_0\big(z,v\big)\Big]= =\delta\int_{\R^d}dz\int_{\R^d}dv\,\overline{\phi(z+v/2)}\phi(z-v/2)\int_0^1ds\,\big[F_\theta(z)\cdot\big(\partial_zK_0\big)\big(z+s\delta\,F_\theta(z),v\big)\big],\end{equation} \begin{equation}\big\langleW[K_\delta]^\circ\,,\,(\overline{\phi}\otimes\phi)\circ\Upsilon^{-1}\big\rangle_{\mathscr{S}(\R^d\times\R^d)}-\big\langleK_0\,,\,(\overline{\phi}\otimes\phi)\circ\Upsilon^{-1}\big\rangle_{\mathscr{S}(\R^d\times\R^d)}= =\Big|\int_{\R^d}du\int_{\R^d}dz\,\mW_\kappa(z-u)\int_{\R^d}dv\,\overline{\phi(z+v/2)}\phi(z-v/2)\,\Big[K_0\big(z+\delta F_\theta(u),v\big)-K_0\big(z,v\big)\Big]\Big|= =\delta\int_{\R^d}dz\int_{\R^d}dv\,\overline{\phi(z+v/2)}\phi(z-v/2)\,\Big[\int_{\R^d}du\,\mW_\kappa(z-u)\,\times \times\,\Big(\int_0^1ds\,\big[F_\theta(u)\cdot\big(\partial_zK_0\big)\big(z+s\delta\,F_\theta(u),v\big)\big]\Big)\Big]\end{equation} {\ We estimate the above two differences in the next two Lemmas.}
elements.sent-2411-078: The symbol $a'_{\delta,\theta (z,\eta)$ associated to the kernel $\mathfrak{K _{\delta,\theta '(z,v):=\int_0^1ds\,\big[F_\theta(z)\cdot\big(\partial_z\mathfrak{K _0\big)\big(z+s\delta\,F_\theta(z),v\big)\big]$ belongs to $S^0_{0,0 (\R^d\times\R^d)$ with seminorms bounded by $C\theta^{-1}$ uniformly for $(\delta,\theta)\in(0,1]^2$.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-080: The symbol $a''_{\delta,\theta (z,\eta)$ associated to the kernel \begin{equation}K_{\delta,\theta}''(z,v):=\int_{\R^d}du\,\mW_\kappa(z-u)\,\Big(\int_0^1ds\,\big[F_\theta(u)\cdot\big(\partial_zK_0\big)\big(z+s\delta\,F_\theta(u),v\big)\big]\Big)\end{equation} belongs to $S^0_{0,0 (\R^d\times\R^d)$ with seminorms bounded by $C\theta^{-1}$ uniformly for $(\delta,\theta)\in(0,1]^2$.}}
elements.sent-2411-083: Putting the above results together we conclude that: \beq \Big|\big\langle\mathfrak{K ^\circ_\delta\,,\,(\overline{\phi}\otimes\phi)\circ\Upsilon^{-1}\big\rangle_{\mathscr{S (\R^d\times\R^d)}\,-\,\big\langle\mW[\mathfrak{K ^\circ_\delta]\,,\,(\overline{\phi}\otimes\phi)\circ\Upsilon^{-1}\big\rangle_{\mathscr{S (\R^d\times\R^d)}\Big|\,\leq\,C(F)\delta\theta^{-1 .}}}}}
